query: requirements for deferred defects under MEL

response.explanation:
Deferred Defects under the Minimum Equipment List (MEL)

  • Definition
    Deferred defects are those reported in operational service that are postponed for later rectification. (Chunk 2)

  • Identification & Recording
    All deferred defects must be identified, recorded, and deferred in accordance with approved procedures and within the approved time limits. (Chunk 1)

  • Pilot/Flight‑Crew Notification
    Every deferred defect should be made known to the pilot or flight crew whenever possible before the aircraft is flown. (Chunk 3, 5)

  • Transfer to Maintenance
    Deferred defects are transferred onto worksheets at the next appropriate maintenance check. (Chunk 3)

  • Re‑entry if Unrectified
    If a deferred defect is not rectified during that maintenance check, it must be re‑entered on a new deferred‑defect record sheet while retaining the original date of the defect. (Chunk 3, 5)

  • Parts Availability
    The necessary components or parts for rectification must be ordered on a priority basis and fitted at the earliest opportunity. (Chunk 3)

  • Listing on Deferred‑Maintenance List
    Deferred defects are listed on the current list of deferred maintenance (ML.A.305(d)(6)) and must be rectified at the next appropriate maintenance event within the limits specified in the maintenance data. (Chunk 5)

  • MEL Application Responsibility
    The MEL is a document not controlled by the Continuing Airworthiness Management Organisation (CAMO). The decision to accept or reject operation with a deferred defect is normally the responsibility of the operating crew. (Chunk 4)

  • Continuous Monitoring
    The CAMO defect‑control system must continuously monitor deferred and carried‑forward defects, ensuring that any significant or repetitive issues are highlighted and addressed. (Chunk 2)

These points summarize the key requirements for handling deferred defects under the MEL, as derived from the retrieved regulatory excerpts.
